## Pull Request Template

### 📝 Description

Provide a clear and concise description of the changes introduced by this pull request. Explain the motivation and any relevant context.

### 🛠️ Changes

Please categorize your changes according to the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) specification. Fill in the relevant sections below:

#### ✨ feat (Features)

_Add new features or significant enhancements here._

- Example: Added user authentication module.

#### 🐛 fix (Bug Fixes)

_List bug fixes and patches here._

- Example: Fixed login redirect issue.

#### 🏗️ build (Build System)

_Include changes that affect the build system or external dependencies._

- Example: Updated Vite configuration for production builds.

#### ♻️ refactor (Refactoring)

_Mention code changes that neither fix a bug nor add a feature._

- Example: Refactored folder structure for better maintainability.

#### 🔧 chore (Chores)

_Routine tasks, maintenance, or other changes that do not modify src or test files._

- Example: Updated dependencies, cleaned up comments.

### 🚀 What's New?

Summarize the most important new features, improvements, or fixes included in this PR.

### ✅ How Has This Been Tested?

Briefly describe how you tested your changes. Include details about the environment, commands used, manual or automated tests, and relevant results.

### 🔗 Related Issues

Reference related issues using keywords (Closes, Fixes, Resolves) and the issue number. Example: Closes #123

### ☑️ Checklist

- [ ] My commit messages follow the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) specification.
- [ ] I have tested the changes locally.
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation.
- [ ] I have reviewed the code for potential security issues.
- [ ] The code follows the project's style guidelines.
- [ ] Existing tests pass successfully.
- [ ] New tests have been added if necessary.

### 🖼️ Screenshots (if applicable)

Attach images or gifs that help illustrate visual changes.

### 🗒️ Additional Notes

Include any extra information that should be considered during the review.

---

> [!NOTE]
> Please ensure your commit messages follow the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) standard for easier tracking and automated releases.
